feed.title: Daily Digest
feed.subtitle: Quote, fact, life hack, fortune cookie, and headlines of the day, plus Polish public-holiday reminders

entry.title: The Uplift: The American Dream
entry.content: We celebrate the United States ahead of its 250th anniversary with stories about American heroes and pastimes. The founder of e.l.f. Cosmetics shares how he made a massive life change to become a Catholic priest.

entry.title: Mississippi teachers say new state-mandated process for buying classroom supplies is more difficult
entry.content: Teachers in Mississippi say accessing state funds for classroom supplies has become more difficult with a new state-mandated process

entry.title: Trump administration caps how long international students can stay in the US
entry.content: The Trump administration has finalized a rule that will prevent international students from staying in the U.S. for more than four years unless they seek and obtain government approval, a departure from long-standing practice

entry.title: Ghost gun company ordered to pay $100M in death of Kentucky teen in historic verdict
entry.content: A ghost gun company has been ordered to pay over $100 million in the death of a Kentucky teenager who had purchased the company's pistol-building kit online

entry.title: NYC helicopter that crashed, killed 6 last year showed signs of a bird strike: NTSB
entry.content: The National Transportation Safety Board has reported that the remains of several geese were found on the wreckage of a sightseeing helicopter that crashed into the Hudson River last year in New York City and killed 6 people

entry.title: Todd Blanche Describes the Huge, Unprecedented Favors Granted by Trump's IRS 'Settlement' as 'Typical'
entry.content: Todd Blanche Describes the Huge, Unprecedented Favors Granted by Trump's IRS 'Settlement' as 'Typical'

entry.title: Tillis says he won't support Blanche unless he meets with Epstein survivors
entry.content: Sen. Thom Tillis said that Todd Blanche must meet with survivors of convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein's crimes before he'll vote to advance his nomination for attorney general out of the Senate Judiciary Committee.

entry.title: Dallas Fed President Logan calls for 'modestly' higher interest rates
entry.content: The policymaker said this week's good inflation news wasn't good enough.

entry.title: Teleprompter operator made nearly $100K betting on Trump speeches
entry.content: After detecting suspicious activity on the teleprompter operator's account, Kalshi investigated and then referred the case to federal regulators.

entry.title: How do AI data centers impact the environment?
entry.content: New York has become the first state to impose a temporary moratorium on data center development while state lawmakers lay the groundwork to assess environmental and social impacts. Dr. Mike Weinstein, the director of sustainability at Southern New Hampshire University, explains what we know about the wider environmental impact data centers.

entry.title: Sen. Tillis says he won't confirm Blanche as attorney general until he meets with Epstein survivors
entry.content: Republican Sen. Thom Tillis said Thursday that he would not confirm Todd Blanche as attorney general until Blanche meets with Epstein survivors.

entry.title: Dozens rescued in Texas after torrential rain triggers flash flooding
entry.content: More than a foot of rain has fallen since Monday, triggering dangerous flash flooding​ in central Texas.

entry.title: 2 executions scheduled for same day in Florida for the first time in more than 60 years
entry.content: The state of Florida is set to execute two death row inmates on the same day for the first time in more than 60 years, now that a stay has been lifted for a former police officer who had been scheduled to die earlier his year

entry.title: Sons say they learned father was killed by ICE through video, news reports
entry.content: Ronaldo Salgado said he learned his father, 52-year-old Mexican immigrant Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, had been shot by an ICE officer in Houston last week through a video posted online that depicted him "screaming" for help.

entry.title: Short sellers load up against SpaceX as stock drops below IPO price
entry.content: About 185 million SpaceX shares are now sold short, representing roughly 29% of the company's publicly tradable float, according to S3.

entry.title: Manu Kone: Man United in pole position to sign Roma midfielder
entry.content: AS Roma midfielder Manu Kone is emerging as Manchester United’s preferred target for a defensive-minded role.Kone, 25, was one of France’s most promising players at the World Cup, impressing with ...

entry.title: Alabama's Nate Oats speaks to dire situation for high school recruits
entry.content: The Crimson Tide's head coach didn't paint a pretty picture for high school hoops recruits moving forward.

entry.title: What Tyrese Haliburton and LeBron said on 'Mind The Game' podcast
entry.content: Tyrese Haliburton joined LeBron James on the 'Mind The Game' podcast; here's what they said about free agency and LeBron James' next team

entry.title: Oil giant BP shutters its corporate venture arm after 20 years
entry.content: BP Ventures is shutting down, ending a nearly 20 year run that was marked by reportedly lackluster returns.

entry.title: NHL unveils its 2026-27 schedule, the first with 84 games apiece since 1993-94
entry.content: The most expansive season in NHL history will begin in September, not October, and limit most teams to four preseason games.

entry.title: Former Vikings head coach considered among the best all-time
entry.content: Bud Grant's legendary Vikings career earned him recognition as one of the NFL's 20 greatest head coaches of all time.

entry.title: The banter never ends! Cup final to open a Série D showdown
entry.content: América-RN and Gama face off this Sunday (19) at Arena das Dunas in the round of 16 of the Brasileirão Série D.So far, so good! Brazil’s lower divisions didn’t stop during the World Cup.But the...

entry.title: Davide Ancelotti steps out of Carlo’s shadow at Lille: ‘I’m here to write my own story’
entry.content: Davide Ancelotti has been unveiled as Lille’s new head coach, and the 36-year-old made clear he carries one of football’s most famous surnames as a privilege rather than a burden.With quotes via I...

entry.title: Julia Garner and Foster the People frontman Mark Foster separate after almost six years of marriage
entry.content: Julia Garner and Mark Foster, frontman of Foster the People, are separating after almost six years of marriage. The 'Ozark' star was recently seen in Los Angeles and Paris without her wedding ring.

entry.title: Is 'The Catcher in the Rye' phony or still cool? 6 writers reflect on its legacy at 75
entry.content: We loved Holden Caulfield at 16, but how does the book hold up? Six California writers unpack the enduring power of Salinger's only novel as it turns 75.

entry.title: Uber’s $14.8B Delivery Hero deal would nearly double its global footprint
entry.content: Uber has agreed to acquire Delivery Hero in a $14.8 billion all-stock deal that would nearly double the company’s global footprint and create one of the world’s largest food delivery platforms outside China.

entry.title: U.S. designates cartel on Texas border as foreign terrorist organization
entry.content: The Juárez Cartel and Los Viagras joined six other Mexican criminal organizations that the U.S. considers terrorist groups.

entry.title: Climate extremes fuel explosive wildfires in Canada and western U.S.
entry.content: One climatologist said "a perfect storm" of climate extremes primed the western U.S. for one of its worst fire seasons in a decade. Meanwhile, Canadian wildfire smoke fills the air.

entry.title: Husband of Code Pink founder being investigated under federal agent and tax laws
entry.content: Neville Roy Singham, who lives in Shanghai, China, is a major financial backer of a New York City-based nonprofit called the People's Forum, a left-leaning organization advocating for causes that affect the working class.

entry.title: Newsletter platform Beehiiv now lets subscribers chat with each other, adds AI
entry.content: Beehiiv is launching an AI Copilot to help publishers with user growth and analytics

entry.title: Big Tech earnings will put focus on AI spending
entry.content: Big Tech earnings will put focus on AI spending

entry.title: Chevron Will Explore Creating Strait of Hormuz Alternative for Iraqi Oil
entry.content: The U.S. energy producer is looking at investing in oil fields and a pipeline to boost Iraq’s oil exports.

entry.title: X cracks down on creators who steal content
entry.content: X will use Grok AI to better detect stolen content, redirect payouts to original creators, and crack down on engagement bait.

entry.title: U.S. and Iran launch new attacks and hurl threats as war intensifies
entry.content: Iran said it would attack "all infrastructure in the region" if President Trump follows through on his threats to attack Iranian civilian infrastructure.

entry.title: AI-powered travel agency Fora hits unicorn status, raises $60M
entry.content: Travel agency Fora announced a $60 million Series D round led by Forerunner and Tactile Ventures, valuing the company at $1 billion.

entry.title: H.R. McMaster on the U.S.-Iran feud over the Strait of Hormuz
entry.content: H.R. McMaster, a CBS News contributor and President Trump's former national security adviser in his first term, joins CBS News 24/7 Mornings to discuss the current situation in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S.-Iran feud ramps up.

entry.title: Google continues its renaming streak by turning NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook
entry.content: Google said users can soon access their notebooks through AI Mode in Search.

entry.title: Google’s AI Mode now lets you link and interact with select apps
entry.content: With this new update, Google is expanding AI Mode beyond answering questions and into completing tasks across the apps they use regularly.

entry.title: Suspects arrested in the Philippines over killing of US marine biologist
entry.content: Philippine police say they have arrested the alleged gunman and two other suspects in connection with the killing of a distinguished American marine biologist in his house in the central Philippines

entry.title: Offer teenagers a meningitis B vaccine on NHS, advisers tell UK government
entry.content: JCVI says children should have one or two doses of menB vaccine at age 15, depending on if they had one as a babyTeenagers should be offered a vaccine on the NHS to protect against deadly meningitis B (menB), government advisers have recommended.The move by the Joint Committee on Vaccination and Immunisation (JCVI), which needs approval from the government, means young people should get a jab around the age of 15, with catch-up programmes for those who would otherwise miss out. Continue reading...

entry.title: Bills RB James Cook's trade price is lower than expected
entry.content: Cook is the leader of Buffalo's backfield.

entry.title: If Bills trade Josh Allen, this would be the mind-blowing cost
entry.content: It'd take a crazy offer to pry the QB from Buffalo.

entry.title: EU forces Google to share search data and open Android to rival AI companies
entry.content: The European Union has issued new rules for Google, requiring it to share search data and open its Android system to rival AI companies

entry.title: Maple Leafs continue to have more reason to keep Matthew Knies amid trade rumors
entry.content: Knies is looking like more of a value as time goes on.

entry.title: It just got a lot harder for Cowboys to trade George Pickens
entry.content: Dallas seems to be content to keep its standout wide receiver around.

entry.title: British Open live golf scores, results, highlights from Thursday's Open Championship Round 1
entry.content: The Sporting News is tracking live scoring updates and hole-by-hole highlights for round one of the 2026 British Open. Follow along for updates from Royal Birkdale.

entry.title: Bills' radio decision is a savage move toward Jets, Giants, Dolphins
entry.content: Buffalo is making sure all of Bills Mafia can tune in.

entry.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: UnitedHealth, TSMC, GE Aerospace, J.B. Hunt & more
entry.content: These are the stocks posting the largest moves in the premarket.

entry.title: Was Lionel Messi offside? Viral images miss key point about Argentina's winning goal vs. England
entry.content: You've got to look at this from a different angle, literally.

entry.title: Jets' Cole Perfetti move may help in a Sabres trade for Connor Hellebuyck
entry.content: Winnipeg is getting other business done in the meantime.

entry.title: Hundreds of Rohingya Feared Dead on World’s Riskiest Migrant Sea Route
entry.content: Two crowded ships sank within days of each other while attempting the perilous crossing toward Malaysia and Indonesia.

entry.title: Uganda calls for travel restrictions to be lifted after last Ebola patient discharged
entry.content: Country begins 42-day countdown to outbreak being declared officially over, as numbers continue to rise in neighbouring Democratic Republic of the CongoUganda has started lobbying countries to lift Ebola-related travel restrictions after discharging its last confirmed Ebola patient from hospital.The discharge of a Congolese national from the Mulago national referral hospital’s isolation centre in Kampala on Thursday triggered the start of a 42-day countdown required by the World Health Organization before Uganda can officially be declared Ebola-free, provided no new infections are detected. Continue reading...

entry.title: Arsenal’s eliminated England stars can’t return home yet
entry.content: England suffered elimination from the World Cup on Wednesday night, but Arsenal’s English internationals can’t return home just yet.Photo by Richard Pelham/Getty ImagesArsenal’s England internat...

entry.title: Newcastle United sign Brighton & Hove Albion defender McLauchlan
entry.content: Newcastle United have completed the signing of Brighton & Hove Albion defender Rachel McLauchlan on a permanent deal.WSL Full-Time were informed of the news via an official club press release from The...

entry.title: New LeMoyne-Owen AD Roderick Smothers Jr. eyes revenue sharing
entry.content: New athletic director Roderick Smothers Jr. plans to implement a revenue-sharing program at LeMoyne-Owen College.

entry.title: Is there a Penguins trade to be made with the Anaheim Ducks?
entry.content: The Pittsburgh Penguins have the salary cap space to help the Anaheim Ducks. They should explore it.

entry.title: Cleveland Browns 2026 training camp position preview: Quarterbacks
entry.content: The Browns open training camp with a quarterback competition between Deshaun Watson and Shedeur Sanders. Here's a look at the position group.

entry.title: Urban Meyer names 5 coaches to watch in 2026 — and Ryan Day isn't one of them
entry.content: Urban Meyer revealed his top five coaches to watch for the 2026 season but notably excluded Ryan Day.

entry.title: Don't miss these 5 things at the Pro Football HOF Festival in Canton
entry.content: There's no shortage of cool and fun things to do at the Pro Football HOF Festival. From food to music to celebs. Here's what you shouldn't miss. 👀

entry.title: Kylian Mbappe next World Cup match: Fixture schedule, start time, draw bracket as France face England
entry.content: Is Kylian Mbappe playing today? The Real Madrid star returns to his favorite stage this summer, with France once again among the leading contenders for glory at the 2026 World Cup.

entry.title: Cyclospora is easy for doctors to miss. The US made it even harder to spot | Robert B Shpiner
entry.content: Last year the federal government downgraded active surveillance for this parasite. Now thousands are sickA patient arrives after two weeks of relentless watery diarrhea, sometimes 20 episodes a day. She has lost weight and cannot keep fluids down. Her stool tests come back negative. Unless someone thinks to order an assay that includes Cyclospora cayetanensis, she may leave without a diagnosis and stay ill for weeks longer.Cyclospora is easy to miss in a clinic. Many routine stool tests do not include it, so a clinician must consider the parasite before the laboratory will look for it. You find it on purpose, or you do not find it.Robert B Shpiner is a clinical professor of medicine in pulmonary and critical care at the David Geffen School of Medicine at UCLA Continue reading...

entry.title: Protesters rally in Kyiv as Zelenskyy moves to oust Ukraine's defense minister
entry.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has moved to dismiss Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov

entry.title: Taiwan computer chipmaker TSMC pledges another $100B to expand US chipmaking capacity
entry.content: Major Taiwan computer chipmaker TSMC says it plans to spend another $100 billion on expanding its manufacturing capacity in the United States

entry.title: To Catch Europe’s Most Elusive Criminals, Police Look to U.S. DNA Tactics
entry.content: Plus, the artificial-intelligence backlash has tech executives fearing for their lives, and Wall Street traders are having their best year ever.

entry.title: ‘Malvinas are Argentinian’: World Cup holders celebrate win over England with Falklands banner
entry.content: Banner refers to Falkland Islands conflict in 1982UK business secretary calls for Fifa investigation The Argentina players celebrated their World Cup win over England with a banner saying “Las Malvinas son Argentinas”, making reference to the 1982 Falklands war.Argentina were 1-0 down with five minutes to go of the semi-final in Atlanta but rallied and scored twice in quick succession to reach a second straight World Cup final, where they will face Spain in New Jersey on Sunday. Continue reading...

entry.title: China Wields Security Laws to Cow Hong Kong’s Once-Free Literary Scene
entry.content: Hong Kong once published books on topics such as the Tiananmen crackdown. But new arrests show Beijing intends to bring the city’s booksellers and publishers to heel.

entry.title: Brazil condemns US move to impose 25% tariffs next week
entry.content: Trade office cites investigation of Brazilian trade practices while Marco Rubio says Lula’s policies ‘bad for Americans and bad for Brazilians’ Brazil has condemned the US decision to impose 25% tariffs on certain Brazilian products, after the Trump administration found a range of what it deemed unfair trade practices by the South American country.“The Brazilian government repudiates the decision announced today by the United States government regarding the imposition of 25 percent tariffs on Brazilian products,” the office of President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva said in a statement on X, denying it had engaged in unfair trade practices. Continue reading...

entry.title: More than 500 feared dead as officials investigate reports of boats capsizing with Rohingya refugees
entry.content: More than 500 feared dead as officials investigate reports of boats capsizing with Rohingya refugees

entry.title: Iran Allows U.S. Citizen It Held for More Than a Year to Leave
entry.content: Trump calls the release a good will gesture amid a deepening standoff over Strait of Hormuz.

entry.title: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom
entry.content: Jordan's military says it shot down 8 missiles launched by Iran targeting the kingdom

entry.title: Moroccan intelligence insider reveals widespread use of Pegasus hacking software
entry.content: Whistleblower suggests internal security services deployed spyware from 2017 against key domestic and foreign targetsA former member of Morocco’s domestic intelligence service has helped to provide an unprecedented insight into how the north African state used hacking software – including Pegasus spyware – to target journalists, human rights defenders, French politicians and Spanish cabinet ministers and police officers.Pegasus, which is manufactured by the Israel-based NSO Group, allows its operator to access everything on a target’s mobile phone, including emails, text messages and photographs. It can also activate the phone’s recorder and camera, turning it into a listening device. Continue reading...

entry.title: Applied Computing wants to give oil and gas operators an AI model for the entire plant
entry.content: Applied Computing has raised a $20M Series A to build a foundation AI model for the oil, gas and petrochemical industry.

entry.title: Lululemon backs nylon-recycling startup Syntetica in $30M Series A
entry.content: Syntetica, a French startup that has developed a novel approach to recycling nylon, has already obtained big-name partners and investors.

entry.title: Canadian Wildfire Smoke Blankets U.S. Northeast and Midwest
entry.content: Conditions are set to worsen in Maryland, Washington, D.C., and Virginia, forecasters said.

entry.title: Trump’s Man in Canada Practices a No-Holds-Barred Diplomacy
entry.content: Ambassador Pete Hoekstra is the president’s unabashed defender in a country where tariffs and talk of a 51st state are wildly unpopular.

entry.title: The AI Backlash Has Tech Executives Fearing for Their Lives
entry.content: Violent threats against artificial-intelligence companies are rising and spilling over into real-world security incidents.

entry.title: How French Police Skirted European Privacy Laws to Hunt a Serial Rapist
entry.content: France and other nations debate allowing police to comb commercial DNA sites to hunt elusive criminals, worrying privacy-rights groups.

entry.title: 'You're scaring my people.' Shia LaBeouf's alleged stalker arrested after posting viral video
entry.content: Hours after posting a video of Shia LaBeouf telling her 'you're scaring my people,' a woman who runs an Instagram account with more than 5,000 posts about the actor was arrested.

entry.title: Paramount prevails in bid for new judge in federal antitrust case
entry.content: Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín will now oversee the high-profile antitrust case challenging Paramount's proposed takeover of Warner Bros.

entry.title: 'Batman 2' is delayed again. Here's everything we know so far about Matt Reeves' sequel
entry.content: Warner Bros. has pushed back the release of 'The Batman Part II' once again. Here is everything to know about Matt Reeves' sequel to his 2022 reboot.

entry.title: Why Greylock capped its new fund at $1.5B when it says it could have raised more
entry.content: By keeping the number of investments to about 25 per fund, Greylock aims to remain what it calls "the most important partner" to its founders.

entry.title: Argentina beats England to reach finals
entry.content: Argentina will now face Spain in the World Cup final after coming from behind to win today’s match. The reigning champs are now fighting for a chance to win back to back titles. NBC News’ Jesse Kirsch has the story.

entry.title: Controlling Hormuz Would Take More U.S. Troops—and Even More Risk
entry.content: As U.S. strikes on Iran continue, military experts say opening the strait would require months of effort.

entry.title: Fortune Cookie of the Day
entry.content:
The one you love is closer than you think.
Lucky Numbers: 82, 94, 95, 77, 98, 7

entry.title: Life Hack of the Day
entry.content: If you wanna get good at something – Do it 100 Times over 100 days.

entry.title: Useless Fact of the Day
entry.content: The plastic things on the end of shoelaces are called aglets.

entry.title: Quote of the Day — Lao Tzu
entry.content: “Knowing others is wisdom, knowing yourself is enlightenment.” — Lao Tzu

entry.title: Microsoft is reportedly training salespeople to talk down OpenAI and Anthropic
entry.content: Microsoft is looking to sell its in-house AI models as more efficient and cost-effective than its competitors' models.

entry.title: Zelensky Ousts Popular Defense Minister as Ukraine Gains Momentum in the War
entry.content: The dismissal provoked criticism among Ukrainians who credit Mykhailo Fedorov for his efforts to modernize the country’s armed forces.

entry.title: New audio from Nolan Wells case
entry.content: NBC News has obtained audio as a part of a records request that details some of Nolan Wells’ friends calling for towing assistance when their boat malfunctioned. NBC News’ Aaron Gilchrist reports from Mississippi on the latest in the college football player’s death investigation.

entry.title: Vance Ramps Up Criticism of Israel
entry.content: In an interview with the podcaster Joe Rogan, the vice president said some in Israel want the war with Iran to continue “indefinitely.”

entry.title: Attorneys for Nolan Wells' family agree to joint inspection of teen's recovered cellphone
entry.content: Attorneys for Nolan Wells' family said they will work with the local district attorney's office investigating his death to inspect the contents of his cellphone. The family recovered the phone from his friends after he was reported missing during a July 4 boat trip to an island off the Mississippi coast.

entry.title: Watch Jude Bellingham slap Argentina player in the head after blowing World Cup semifinal
entry.content: Here's a look at England's star, who appeared to hit 21-year-old Valentin Barco after the loss.

entry.title: Three people still missing, one dead after boat capsizes near Alcatraz Island
entry.content: A boat carrying 20 people capsized and sank near Alcatraz Island, killing one person and, according to city officials, injuring three people with three others still missing.

entry.title: Black doctors in England four times less likely to get training places than white counterparts
entry.content: For one placement, black applicants had a less than 1 in 100 chance of being offered a place, NHS data showsBlack doctors in England are four times less likely to be offered a training place than their white counterparts, according to analysis.As part of their medical training, doctors across the NHS are able to apply to placements within specific branches of practice such as psychiatry, obstetrics and gynaecology, and emergency medicine. Continue reading...

entry.title: England vs. Argentina player ratings: Live grades for Lionel Messi, Thomas Tuchel, others in 2026 World Cup semifinal
entry.content: Lionel Messi and Argentina were five minutes from exiting the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but Thomas Tuchel and England threw away a spot in the World Cup final as they fell to the defending champions.

entry.title: Worst England World Cup heartbreaks: Where devastating Argentina loss ranks among most disappointing results
entry.content: England has suffered its fair share of devastating losses during the World Cup. Wednesdeay's loss ot Argenitna might be the most tragic of those results.

entry.title: Fetterman warns he would leave Democratic Party if it turns its back on Israel
entry.content: Fetterman warns he would leave Democratic Party if it turns its back on Israel

entry.title: How cowardly Thomas Tuchel threw away England's World Cup in semifinal collapse to Argentina
entry.content: It seemed that England had one foot in the World Cup final. They did, until suddenly they didn't, and Thomas Tuchel has only himself to blame.

entry.title: 2026 World Matchplay of Darts prize money: Details on increased amounts for winner and others
entry.content: Players will be able to earn more this year than they did in Blackpool in 2025.
